title: PromotionGate integration: operator review required before any promotion
goal: Wire the self-improvement loop's AcceptRejectDecision stage through the KERNEL-001 PromotionGate so any Accept (a) requires operator review evidence before the editable surface mutation is applied to authority and (b) emits a typed promotion receipt that EventLedger replays can reconstruct. No promotion proceeds without operator signoff per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS ('PromotionGate operator review per promotion').
expected_diff_shape: New module src/backend/handshake_core/src/self_improve/promotion_gate_adapter.rs (LoopPromotionGate + PromotionRequest + PromotionApproval + PromotionRejection types). Wiring uses existing KERNEL-001 PromotionGate trait surface; no new gate. Tests src/backend/handshake_core/tests/loop_promotion_gate_tests.rs.
unblock_conditions: MT-148 (loop core), MT-149 (editable surface), MT-152 (promotion floor). KERNEL-001 PromotionGate exists in main.
implementation_notes: Per AC-DISTILL-LOOP-SAFEGUARDS: 'PromotionGate operator review per promotion'. Per refinement D.2: 'promotion-gated; operator review required before promotion'. Surface: `pub struct LoopPromotionGate<'a> { gate: &'a dyn PromotionGateSubmitter }` with `pub fn submit(request: PromotionRequest) -> Result<PromotionTicket, GateError>` and `pub fn poll(ticket: &PromotionTicket) -> Result<PromotionStatus, GateError>`. PromotionRequest: `{ iteration_id, target: LoopTarget, baseline_snapshot, proposed_snapshot, eval_result: EvalResult, floor_decision: PromotionDecision, sentinel_decision: SentinelDecision, justification_text: String }`. PromotionStatus: `{ Pending { submitted_at_utc }, Approved { approval: PromotionApproval }, Rejected { rejection: PromotionRejection } }`. PromotionApproval: `{ approved_by: OperatorId, approved_at_utc, signoff_evidence_id: Uuid v7 }`. PromotionRejection: `{ rejected_by: OperatorId, rejected_at_utc, rejection_reason: String }`. The gate is asynchronous (submit then poll) because operator review may take hours/days; the loop scheduler (MT-156) tracks pending tickets and resumes the iteration ONLY after Approved. On Rejected, the loop records the rejection as a memory entry (so future iterations learn) and advances to the next target. Tests: submit creates a typed request via the gate; poll returns Pending immediately after submit; gate rejection produces typed PromotionRejection; gate approval populates signoff_evidence_id; loop cannot apply the surface mutation without an Approved status (assert by attempting apply with Pending status returns typed error).
risk_if_missed: Without PromotionGate integration the loop can apply Accept decisions directly to authority, bypassing operator review and creating a shadow self-modification path.
validator_focus: PromotionRequest captures the full evidence bundle (baseline + proposal + eval + floor + sentinel). Apply path is gated by PromotionStatus::Approved.

minimum_controls: Apply path is gated by PromotionStatus::Approved; Pending or Rejected returns typed error.
PromotionRequest bundles all evidence (baseline + proposal + eval + floor + sentinel) so operator review is informed.
Rejection is recorded as memory so future iterations do not re-propose the same change.
